notes: Phase 4 pilots are bounded experiments, not architecture changes.
Keep pilot scope narrow enough that Phase 5 can evaluate usefulness without inheriting hidden dependencies.
If a pilot is not clearly safe or clearly useful, defer it with a written reason.

pilots.name: GitHub Delivery Signals
pilots.scope: One read-only synced database pilot for a single active project or repo if a clearly supported source is available.
successCriteria: Improves delivery visibility for one active project
Stays read-only and clearly separate from canonical scoring
Produces evidence that helps judge whether external telemetry should enter Phase 5
constraints: No writeback into GitHub or canonical Notion properties
No expansion beyond one project in Phase 4
Do not let synced rows drive recommendation scores directly
pilots.deferReason: Live rollout is deferred until a single high-value repo and supported synced source are confirmed.

pilots.name: Weekly Native Summary Draft
pilots.scope: One weekly custom-agent draft that reads the latest published review and recommendation context and writes a draft into the native briefs page.
successCriteria: Stays easy to review before use
Adds evidence that helps decide whether custom agents belong in later phases
constraints: Never edits canonical project, execution, or recommendation records
Requires human review before the output is treated as meaningful
pilots.deferReason: Live rollout is deferred until the dashboard layer is stable and the weekly draft workflow is worth the extra native complexity.
